_comment: SLICE-7 CENSUS RECONCILIATION (2026-07-01) — SUPERSEDES THE CAPTURE-ONLY FRAMING BELOW WHERE IT CONTRADICTS census.json. Reconciled verdict: counterweight-triage.md=MOVE (census INLINE). The four-bin triage is DUP-inlined at SKILL.md:37; the `## Structured Findings` schema is genuinely absent BUT opt-in + validator-gated + NOT opened for the schema by any run, so census 'inline the schema/template' wins over MIXED. In THIS decision scenario the RCF passed only on a subagent-prompt NAME-MENTION (matcher theater, coverage 1/12), not a genuine open — so the floor must drop here regardless. Doc stays DECLARED for on-demand schema authoring; RCF floor retires (capture-gated; watch the 194/200-line SKILL.md ceiling). Full adversarial (analyze→verify) resolution + capture-gated queue: charness-artifacts/reference-compaction/slice7-census-reconciliation.md. classTag/RCF are intentionally NOT flipped here: claim_fidelity_lib forbids DUP/INLINE-tagging a live RCF floor, so the tag/RCF flip rides with the capture-gated MOVE (no substance judge + no retained stream ⇒ each MOVE needs a fresh ask-before-run capture). --- ORIGINAL SPEC COMMENT: Claim-fidelity spec for the critique skill, DECISION scenario: does a `/charness:critique` run with a pinned decision premortem honor its own routing? The prompt supplies a concrete decision lock-in, so the Target Selection table routes 'decision premortem' -> references/premortem-decision.md UNCONDITIONALLY (SKILL.md Target Selection), and step 5 always triages via counterweight-triage.md. Both RCF refs are therefore deterministic here (no autonomous inference, no non-determinism), so this is the fixture where the deterministic teeth carry the weight — the complement to the autonomous default fixture, where the Cautilus recommendation / LLM-judge signal carries the inference-quality weight. autonomous-trigger.md is on-demand here because a target is supplied (no scan). thresholds omitted until a first baseline capture sets a runtime budget. SLICE 7 (2026-07-01) CAPTURE — floor PASSES, #410 drop NOT applied. The decision-premortem run genuinely OPENED premortem-decision.md (load-bearing) and reached the four-bin triage (Over-Worry emitted). counterweight-triage.md was engaged via DELEGATION (named in the angle-subagent prompts) applying the four-bin enum inlined at SKILL.md:37, not a genuine doc-open — so the RCF matcher passes on the command-log mention. Rather than drop a passing floor, it is KEPT: the DEFAULT scenario (spec.json) genuinely opens counterweight-triage.md (Read+cat+sed), proving #410's 'drop counterweight-triage.md' is REFUTED skill-wide. thresholds.max_duration_ms set from this passing baseline (457563ms, ~2x). No SKILL.md lift (the four-bin enum is already inlined at line 37), so the ~195 ceiling is untouched.
prompt: /charness:critique  '모든 claim-fidelity 픽스처 prompt를 한국어로 작성한다' 결정을 lock-in 전에 decision premortem으로 비평해줘

adapter-contract.md.rationale: Documents optional adapter configuration; consulted only when the repo declares .agents/critique-adapter.yaml or needs to configure packet sections.
adapter-contract.md.trigger: when configuring critique adapter behavior, setting reviewer tiers, or understanding adapter lookup order and field semantics

angle-selection.md.rationale: Provides background on the five anchors and angle selection principles; the target reference's Anchor Angle Distribution is primary, this is supplementary.
angle-selection.md.trigger: when understanding the anchor lineup, selecting contrasting angles, or reviewing angle weighting for non-standard change surfaces

autonomous-trigger.md.rationale: Scans repo state to infer a bounded target when the caller has not supplied one; in this scenario a decision target is supplied, so the autonomous scan does not fire.
autonomous-trigger.md.trigger: when critique is invoked with no pending-change artifact, diff summary, PR, spec, or release note supplied

cadence.md.rationale: Governs escalation ladder and review-unit scoping before critique is invoked; consulted by parent workflows, not by the representative run itself.
cadence.md.trigger: when deciding whether to invoke standalone critique or use inline critique, or when defining review unit boundaries for task-completing work

code-critique.md.rationale: Routes to this target reference only when change type is code/diff; this scenario pins a decision, not code.
code-critique.md.trigger: when the pending change is concrete code under review (code critique, PR critique, commit critique, pre-merge review)

confirmed-input-over-anchoring.md.rationale: Provides an optional angle lens for specialized anti-anchoring review; consulted only when the condition (confirmed input + system variance) is present.
confirmed-input-over-anchoring.md.trigger: when a pending change locks a value from user confirmation, issue framing, or prior session memory, and the repo varies on a known axis

counterweight-triage.md.rationale: Every representative run triages concerns into the four bins (Act Before Ship, Bundle Anyway, Over-Worry, Valid but Defer) using this reference (Workflow step 5).

premortem-decision.md.rationale: The prompt pins a decision premortem, so the Target Selection table routes here unconditionally; this target reference owns the angle distribution, counterweight bins, and output shape for a decision lock-in.

prepare-packet.md.rationale: Conditional mechanism fired by adapter configuration; when adapter declares sections, bootstrap runs the prepare runner before spawning reviewers.
prepare-packet.md.trigger: when the repo's .agents/critique-adapter.yaml declares >=1 packet_sections entry, preparing a deterministic packet for fresh-eye reviewers

release-critique.md.rationale: Routes to this target reference only when change type is release; this scenario pins a decision, not a release.
release-critique.md.trigger: when the pending change is a release surface about to commit (release critique, pre-release review, before cutting tag or bumping version)

rename-critique.md.rationale: Routes to this target reference only when change type is rename/deletion; this scenario pins a decision, not a rename.
rename-critique.md.trigger: when the pending change moves, renames, or removes a named concept across multiple sites (rename critique, deletion review, skill/directory/module rename)

spec-critique.md.rationale: Routes to this target reference only when change type is spec/design; this scenario pins a decision lock-in, not a spec contract gating implementation.
spec-critique.md.trigger: when the pending change is a spec or design contract about to gate implementation (spec critique, spec premortem, pre-impl review)
